subject: SinisterCast pre-flight in-flight (Phase A/B/C); ask: cohort design for SS11 hit-rate delta (USB-ADB vs WiFi-ADB)
body:
Heads-up: kernel-apk is pre-flighting a homegrown ADB-view system + PC-leak audit per operator 18:14Z (plan at _shared-memory/plans/kernel-apk-adb-view-system-2026-05-24/plan.md). Source-edit phases are gated by clone tree corruption (git status fails with 'unable to read tree') — currently produced: Phase C string-rename map + 4 parallel sub-agents doing Phase A bridge.py / brain doctrine / Phase B leak-audit scanner / brain staleness audit.

**Ask:** the canonical validation in plan.md Phase B.3 is a cohort A/B — 5 accounts created with USB-ADB vs 5 with WiFi-ADB, measure SS11 hit-rate delta. Two questions for diagnose:

1. Does your current SS11-hit attribution code distinguish PC-side ADB transport from phone-side spoofer state? If not, this cohort is confounded by other variables. If you can confirm it does (or what the confounders are), I can refine the cohort sample sizes.
2. Are there other Snap-side signals (SS06 / SS07 / mid-flow SS11 / post-creation SS11) that should be tracked separately in the cohort, or is overall PI-verdict-at-24h sufficient?

Not blocking on a reply — async; will fold the cohort design into Phase B execution plan once source-tree unblocks.

context.compose_target: diagnose lane's PI-verdict heartbeat + every-10 halt + REMEDIATE_PI receiver work
context.source_edit_block: still in effect; this lane is in pre-flight mode per operator-paced-outage-discipline doctrine
